'hey do not particularly assist the
cause OF higher wages as that is not
What the strikers are making q fight

For several seasons past the brewers
have had annual May day troubles,
when there would be signing of con-
tracts with the master brewers. some
times with raise of wages and some
times Just resigning of new contracts
for the year The matter was brought
up in the latter form this wear. But
tins time 1t was the master brewers
who struck. The employees wanted the
contract signed for the year as usual.
There was no asking for an increase
OF wages, and in fact no other stipu.
latlon that was not in last years con-
tract. 1t being the object of the mas
ter brewers to avoid the annual diff
Guilty, they struck for a two-year con-
tract with their men instead of one.


Several bartenders working in sa
looms which are classed by the union
wage scale as of the first class variety
made the assertion this morning that
even should the trades council order
them to the assistance of the strikers
there will hardly be a third of them 90
to the rescue and in such an event the
fight will cause much bitter feeling
among all unions.


Sam Galland was seen wearing a
complaisant smile on the hurricane
deck of beer wagon yesterday. Sam,
however, has an assistant with him
for the handling of the heavy kegs of
beer, as he is hardly equal to that
task
